commit.message:
fix: remove erroneous PR subtraction from issue counts; clamp negatives in display

The issues.listForRepo API returns only issues (not PRs) in its
response, so subtracting PR counts produced large negative issue values
(e.g. -468, -619) which Chart.js rendered as bars extending to the
left, creating an unintended butterfly/diverging chart appearance.

Changes:
- collectIssueCounts: drop the two pulls.list calls and remove the
  subtraction; use the raw issues API counts directly (+ Math.max(0)
  guard for safety)
- build-pages.ts: clamp r.issues.open / r.issues.closed to >= 0 when
  aggregating totals and building topRepos, so any existing cached or
  fixture data with negative values is rendered correctly
- issues.test.ts: update tests to reflect the new single-API approach

fix: use Modifier::BOLD for command names in overlays to force brightness

Style::default() renders as dim on Catppuccin and similar dark color
schemes where the terminal's base foreground color is desaturated.
BOLD reliably renders bright on all terminals regardless of palette.

- SelectionList non-selected label: default() → BOLD
- SelectionList non-selected desc: DIM → default() (visually secondary to bold label)
- InfoPanel /help command names: BOLD (unchanged) + desc: DIM → default()
- Tip lines in InfoPanel: keep DIM (truly secondary info)

* fix: port picking checks both TCP+UDP, add run_setup_with_port_retry helper

- `pick_free_udp_port()` now loops until the chosen port is bindable on
  both UDP and TCP (without SO_REUSEADDR), mirroring what
  `port_manager.probe_live_listener` checks.  This prevents TIME_WAIT
  TCP sockets left by a previous test in the same batch from causing
  "Live listener already holds 0.0.0.0:<port>" failures in setup.
- `pick_free_tcp_port()` gets the same dual-protocol verification.
- New `_port_free_for_both()` private helper centralises the check.
- New `run_setup_with_port_retry()` public helper provides belt-and-
  suspenders: if setup still fails due to a port conflict it parses
  AlphaGSM's "Recommended free port set" line for the next candidate
  (falling back to a fresh `pick_free_tcp_port()`) and retries up to
  max_tries times.
- Local `_pick_free_port()` in test_tf2.py (UDP-only) and
  test_minecraft_vanilla.py (TCP-only) updated with the same dual check.

commit.message:
fix: 3 code review findings from v0.8 diff audit

[CRITICAL] fts.ts: fix surrogate pair corruption for CJK Extension B chars
- buildSearchTokens() and preprocessFtsQuery() previously incremented i by 1
  for all codepoints, but Extension B chars (U+20000-U+2A6DF) need i += 2
- Use String.fromCodePoint(cp) + conditional advance to handle surrogate pairs
- Affects any text containing rare archaic CJK characters (U+20000+)

[HIGH] cache.ts: guard getCachedQuery() against hash collision
- djb2 32-bit hash has birthday paradox collision risk at ~65k queries
- Add query field check: if stored query != requested query, skip cache hit
- Prevents silent cache poisoning from hash collisions

[MEDIUM] query.ts: add ls > 0 check in extractSnippet word-boundary trim
- lastIndexOf(' ') returns -1 when no space found; -1 > length-20 is false
  for normal lengths so no correctness issue, but add guard for clarity

commit.message:
Fix OpenAPI publish job being skipped on manual workflow dispatch (#45)

https://github.com/SubDynamics/event-hub-api/pull/45

The OpenAPI workflow’s `publish-types-package` job could be skipped on
manual runs due to an overly strict branch gate and string-based input
check. This updates job gating so manual dispatch behaves independently
from push-to-main change detection.

- **Condition split by trigger type**
- Kept push behavior scoped to `main` + detected `packages/types/**`
changes.
- Separated manual dispatch logic so it no longer depends on `github.ref
== refs/heads/main`.

- **Typed manual input evaluation**
- Switched from `github.event.inputs.publish == 'true'` to
`inputs.publish` to align with boolean `workflow_dispatch` inputs.

- **Resulting job predicate**
  ```yaml
  if: >
    (github.event_name == 'push' &&
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    needs.generate-and-validate.outputs.types_changed == 'true') ||
    (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.publish)
  ```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SubDynamics <23660334+SubDynamics@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Style::default()+DIM for overlay text to avoid ANSI color remapping

Replace Color::White and MUTED in overlays with terminal-adaptive styles:
- Primary text: Style::default() (inherits terminal's configured fg color)
- Secondary text: Modifier::DIM (dims relative to terminal's native color)

Affected: SelectionList labels/descs/markers, InfoPanel help lines (commands,
tips), status overlay values, and all overlay border hint text.

than true-color Rgb(140,140,140), producing inverted visual hierarchy.

commit.message:
Add step segmentation protocol

Implement hierarchical step segmentation as described in Appendix:
- Priority 1: Structural delimiters (\n\n, \n, \\)
- Priority 2: Logic connectors (Therefore, Thus, Hence, So, Consequently)
- Priority 3: Explicit enumerations (Step N:, 1., First,)
- LaTeX math protection (never split inside $...$)
- Max 256 tokens per step to prevent run-on steps

Integrated into:
- MCTS expand: segment multi-step outputs, keep first step only
- format_trajectory: re-segment before numbering for analyst

commit.message:
fix: dotfile sync via adapter.list + incremental sync speed optimization

Filter fix:
- Remove blanket dotfile ignore from isIgnoredPath; only explicit
  ignorePatterns control filtering (.claudian/**, .omc/**, etc.)
- Add .obsidian/cache to BUILT_IN_IGNORE_PATHS
- Fix matchesPattern for bare names (e.g. .DS_Store) to match at any
  directory level

Adapter fix:
- Use app.vault.adapter.list() to discover ALL files including dotfiles
  (app.vault.getFiles() only returns Obsidian-managed non-hidden files)
- Add walkAdapterFiles() recursive directory walker
- Fallback readChangeContent via adapter.readBinary for hidden files

Speed optimizations (incremental sync: ~34s → ~15s):
- Post-sync page token refresh: async fire-and-forget (was 5s blocking)
- Post-push cursor: compute locally from committed entries (saves 2 API calls)
- Snapshot publish: async for ≤3 changed files (finalize: 5s → <0.1s)
- uploadBlob: remove double findByLogicalPath (saves 1 API call)
- Blob upload + op append: run in parallel via Promise.all
- Skip reconcileUncommittedOps after successful push
- Skip compaction + blob GC for ≤10 changed files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
